title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the rs186045772 AA genotype (two copies of the CFTR F1074L variant) and cystic fibrosis may respond to ivacaftor treatment. FDA-approved drug labeling information and CPIC guidelines indicate use of ivacaftor in cystic fibrosis patients with at least one copy of F1074L. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.
riskSummary: CFTR variant associated with efficacy for ivacaftor
detail: Patients with the rs186045772 AA genotype (two copies of the CFTR F1074L variant) and cystic fibrosis may respond to ivacaftor treatment. title: Likely miscall in 23andMe v4 data; otherwise, Alzheimer's disease pathogenic mutation
summary: Likely miscall in 23andMe v4 data; otherwise, Alzheimer's disease pathogenic mutation
detail: Although reported reasonably credibly in the scientific literature as a dominant mutation leading to Alzheimer's disease, Promethease users with raw data from the 23andMe v4 chip appear to have this genotype reported more frequently than public databases would predict (or than is seen in raw data from other companies). Anyone with follow-on confirmatory testing (positive or negative) for this genotype is encouraged to contact us. See links via rs63749964
